messages.text: Morning. Two things on my plate today — the Sepang steel order and the Johor PPVC decision. ESG audit is next week, carbon numbers have to be clean. Let's get both sorted.
On it. Jane pulled the quotes last night. Tan's been at the Sepang site since 6, he'll have the schedule view.

Morning all. For Sepang we need 15,000,000kg of structural steel beams. Got two quotes — the cheaper one uses virgin steel and regular diesel trucks, comes in at RM 13,500,000. The green option is RM 14,550,000, recycled steel, biofuel delivery. HS Code 7216.32.000 for both.
Delivery matters a lot here. We've got concrete pours locked in for Day 4. If steel shows up late everything moves.

Cheaper option delivers in 3 days, no problem. Green option is 5 days — biofuel fleet doesn't have great coverage up here. It's tight for Day 4. Also we have 8,000,000kg of cement and 5,000,000kg of concrete on order for the foundation works.
Could push the pour to Day 6 if we front-load the precast panels. Adds a day of idle time, maybe 2k in standby costs but not a disaster.

So the question is whether the carbon savings on the green option justify the RM 1,050,000 premium plus the RM 2,000 resequencing cost. @AI can you look up the emission difference between virgin and recycled structural steel for 15,000,000kg and tell us what the carbon tax hit looks like for each option?
Also factor in the ESG audit next week. A bad GHG score could cost us more than a few thousand in penalties and lost scoring. @AI weigh both sides properly.

Moving on — Johor PPVC. We're looking at one module batch for the affordable housing block. Two choices: concrete modules at RM 2,050,000 or steel modules at RM 2,280,000. Also we need 3,000,000kg of aluminium for the facade panels and 1,500,000kg of PVC for the plumbing systems.
Site condition in Johor is soft ground. Steel modules are lighter so we save on foundation work. Concrete needs deeper piling, our estimate is about RM 180,000 extra for that.
So concrete's real upfront cost is closer to RM 2,230,000 once you add piling. Gap narrows to RM 50,000 against steel. And from what I recall steel tends to have better long-term recovery value.
There's also a Malaysian peer study in our LCA database comparing the two for housing projects. I haven't pulled the numbers yet but it should have GHG and energy use figures.
Chow mentioned we might be able to register steel under BCX carbon credits through MGTC. That could offset the higher production emissions. Look into that too.

Yep, it's worth a look before we lock in. @AI , pull the full picture on both PPVC options. Check the LCA database, calculate the carbon tax under CCUS Act 2025, and give us a recommendation Boss can sign off on.
One more thing , for Sepang, is there any lead time buffer we can build in without hurting the overall programme? @AI check if Option B timing is actually a blocker given we can shift the pour.

@AI,read everything above and give me a final call on both Sepang and Johor. I want the carbon numbers from our database, the tax calc, and your recommendation. Make it something I can bring to the board.
